 {"id":"bd-1s6w5","title":"task(doctor-asupersync): add unit, integration, E2E, soak, and replay suites with detailed logging","description":"Background:
`doctor_frankentui` needs exceptionally strong proof because it is the operator-facing diagnostic safety net. Its tests and scripts need to be exhaustive and rich in evidence.

Goal:
Build the comprehensive validation suite for the doctor migration.","design":"Work packages:
- Add unit tests for core orchestration decisions and artifact generation.
- Add integration tests for workflow segments.
- Add E2E happy/failure/cancellation/timeout scripts.
- Add determinism soak and replay-triage coverage.
- Ensure all scripts emit detailed manifests, summaries, and replay pointers.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Validation covers happy path, failure path, determinism soak, cancellation, timeout, fallback, and replay.
2. Script outputs are richly logged and stable enough for CI artifact contracts.
3. Doctor migration is not considered complete until these tests exist and are wired.","notes":"This bead is intentionally heavyweight because `doctor_frankentui` is the project’s confidence engine during failures.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":null,"owner":null,"estimated_minutes":null,"created_at":"2026-03-10T01:34:37.123599389+00:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T01:34:43.722324232+00:00","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null,"closed_by_session":null,"due_at":null,"defer_until":null,"external_ref":null,"source_system":null,"source_repo":".","deleted_at":null,"deleted_by":null,"delete_reason":null,"original_type":null,"compaction_level":0,"compacted_at":null,"compacted_at_commit":null,"original_size":0,"sender":null,"ephemeral":false,"pinned":false,"is_template":false,"labels":["asupersync-frankentui","doctor_frankentui","e2e","replay","soak","test"],"dependencies":[],"comments":[]}

-{"id":"bd-1sder","title":"task(asupersync-architecture): define invariants, user-visible metrics, and canonical evidence schema","description":"Background:
The migration needs explicit invariants and user-visible success metrics. Otherwise validation becomes vague and benchmarks measure the wrong things.

Goal:
Define the invariant set, user/operator metrics, and evidence schema that every later task must trace back to.","design":"Work packages:
- Enumerate correctness invariants: semantic parity, deterministic output, shutdown guarantees, terminal safety, replay identity.
- Enumerate user/operator metrics: effect turnaround, shutdown latency, child-process cancellation latency, failure diagnosability, doctor completion time, artifact completeness.
- Define the canonical evidence schema and correlation IDs that tests and scripts must emit.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every later test and rollout task can point to named invariants and metrics from this bead.
2. Metrics are user-meaningful and operator-meaningful, not only internal counters.
3. The evidence schema is rich enough to drive replay and go/no-go decisions.","notes":"This bead is the backbone for the validation traceability matrix and benchmark gates.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T01:17:31.141235394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T01:18:08.623044164Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync-frankentui","evidence","invariants","metrics"]}

 {"id":"bd-1sm6v","title":"epic(alien-artifact): Galaxy-Brain Transparency Demo Screen","description":"Live UI showing: (1) posterior distributions for all active decisions, (2) top evidence terms with Bayes factors, (3) chosen actions with expected loss, (4) confidence intervals. Force drift then show automatic fallback. The 'killer demo' from FrankenSuite summary. This is what makes FrankenTUI feel like an alien artifact — the math is visible and beautiful.

**User Value:** Interactive galaxy-brain cards let users see exactly why each adaptive decision was made, building trust in the framework.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Decision card widget renders at all 4 disclosure levels
- Live sparkline shows posterior probability evolution
- Demo screen works in both inline and alt-screen modes
- All existing tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:50:12.751206044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:40.109226266Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:40.109203323Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-1lgz8 (existing Killer Demo Galaxy-Brain Transparency epic)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","demo","galaxy-brain"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sm6v","depends_on_id":"bd-fp38v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1sm6v.1","title":"task(e2e): E2E test suite for Galaxy-Brain Demo","description":"## E2E Integration Test: Galaxy-Brain Transparency Demo (EV=killer)

### Scope
Full integration test rendering the galaxy-brain transparency demo screen. Validates that all Bayesian decision visualizations are present, progressive disclosure works, and card rendering is correct.

### Test Scenario
1. Initialize FrankenTUI with galaxy-brain demo screen active.
2. Verify initial render shows all active decision cards:
   - Posterior distribution sparklines for each decision point
   - Top evidence terms with Bayes factors
   - Chosen actions with expected loss
   - Confidence interval bars
3. Trigger drift: inject increasing frame times over 50 frames.
4. Verify automatic fallback visualization appears (decision switches from adaptive to conservative).
5. Test progressive disclosure: collapse/expand each decision card.
6. Verify card rendering at different terminal sizes (80x24, 120x40, 200x60).
7. Test keyboard navigation between decision cards.
8. Verify all 7 decision points from Evidence Ledger are represented.

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Each frame of the demo screen emits:
```json
{
  "event": "galaxy_brain_frame",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "frame_id": u64,
  "cards_visible": u32,
  "cards_expanded": u32,
  "cards_collapsed": u32,
  "decision_points_shown": ["string"],
  "drift_detected": bool,
  "fallback_active": bool,
  "progressive_disclosure_state": {
    "card_id": "string",
    "expanded": bool,
    "sparkline_points": u32
  },
  "terminal_size": { "cols": u16, "rows": u16 },
  "render_time_ns": u64,
  "frame_hash": "hex_string"
}
```

### Success Criteria
- All 7 decision points visible as cards on initial render.
- Drift injection triggers visible fallback indicator within 10 frames of detection.
- Progressive disclosure toggles work (expand/collapse changes card height).
- Sparklines render with at least 20 data points after warm-up.
- Demo renders correctly at all 3 tested terminal sizes.
- No panics, no unsafe code (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]).
- JSONL log is schema-compliant and parseable.
- Frame render time < 16ms (60fps capable).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:50.527061302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:41.311417025Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:41.311397989Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-1lgz8 epic (E2E testing covered by existing epic)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sm6v.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1sm6v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3exa7","title":"Add VT conformance fixtures: erase-with-bg-color + wide-char edit interactions","description":"Add conformance fixtures for two under-tested areas in frankenterm-core:

1. Erase operations with non-default background color:
   - ED 0/1/2 with cursor bg set to a named/indexed/rgb color
   - EL 0/1/2 with cursor bg set
   - ECH with cursor bg set
   - Verify erased cells have the correct background color

2. Wide character + erase/insert/delete interactions:
   - ECH in the middle of a wide char (should clean up the wide char pair)
   - ICH at a position splitting a wide char
   - DCH removing half of a wide char
   - EL right starting in the middle of a wide char

These are common sources of bugs in terminal emulators and directly support the frankenterm-core correctness goals.

Files touched: tests/fixtures/vt-conformance/erase/ and tests/fixtures/vt-conformance/wide_chars/","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleCave","created_at":"2026-02-09T05:26:21.516292416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:30:58.757277409Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:30:58.757258443Z","close_reason":"Added 8 VT conformance fixtures testing erase operations with non-default background colors (EL 0/1/2 + ED 0/1/2 + ECH with named + RGB backgrounds). All pass, quality gates clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-3f24s","title":"task(asupersync-architecture): define dependency boundaries, feature flags, shadow mode, and fallback topology","description":"Background:
We need a migration path that can be enabled and evaluated safely. That means feature flags, runtime selection, shadow-run, and fallback are architectural concerns, not afterthoughts.

Goal:
Define the dependency boundary, cargo feature strategy, runtime selection surface, shadow-run topology, and fallback policy before implementation begins.","design":"Work packages:
- Decide crate boundaries and optional dependency placement.
- Define compile-time and runtime flags.
- Define shadow-run topology for comparing legacy vs Asupersync lanes.
- Define fallback and rollback semantics for runtime and doctor paths.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The plan names exactly how the new lane is enabled, shadowed, disabled, and rolled back.
2. Dependency placement avoids contaminating pure crates unnecessarily.
3. The design supports detailed logging and artifact capture during comparison and rollback drills.","notes":"User-value lens:
A technically correct migration is still bad if operators cannot understand when it is active, compare it, or back it out quickly.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T01:16:35.944104386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T01:17:03.963332707Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync-frankentui","fallback","flags","shadow-run"]}

 {"id":"bd-3fc","title":"Epic: Testing & Conformance Infrastructure","description":"Comprehensive testing infrastructure: unit tests for every module, integration tests for ring boundaries, E2E tests for full render pipeline, property-based tests (section 6.12), deterministic replay via FrankenLab, golden frame checksums for 47+ test scenarios, terminal emulator compatibility matrix (5 emulators), conformance harness, fuzz testing for ANSI parser, performance regression gates with slo.yaml enforcement.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:00.029731605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:00.029731605Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","epic","quality","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-3fc.1","title":"Golden frame checksum infrastructure (BLAKE3)","description":"Infrastructure for golden-frame testing: capture terminal output as byte array, compute BLAKE3 hash, compare against stored golden checksums. 47+ test scenarios covering: all widget types, all terminal emulators, resize, animations, edge cases. Update workflow: cargo test -- --update-golden. Storage: golden checksums in tests/golden/ directory. TRACING: span 'golden.compare' with fields: scenario_name, computed_hash, expected_hash, match. LOG: INFO for golden update, ERROR for mismatch. METRICS: counter golden_tests_run_total, counter golden_mismatches_total.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:11.836995812Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:24:47.297076015Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T04:24:47.297056639Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: BLAKE3 golden frame infrastructure in ftui-harness (golden.rs, golden_resize.rs, golden_checksums.rs, checksum files)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["blake3","conformance","golden","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3fc.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1q5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fc.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3fc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Two machine-validation tests: metrics_jsonl_line_includes_required_fields (validates all field types via serde_json) and metrics_jsonl_quality_fields_populated (validates quality fields are non-null for valid samples). All 86 mermaid_showcase tests pass, clippy clean. Agent: BrightSnow","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:39:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zdbm","title":"PTY integration test: TerminalSession cleanup sequences","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T02:22:51.955647053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:25:12.656720918Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:25:12.656701993Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-zj0es","title":"State: viewport transform behavior","description":"Goal
- Define zoom/fit/pan behavior AND viewport size override behavior.

Deliverables
- Zoom in/out step, reset, fit-to-viewport logic.
- Viewport size override controls (width/height) with clamping.
- Pan offsets clamped to bounds if applicable.

Acceptance
- Viewport behavior is deterministic and consistent across sizes.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"subtask","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:04:31.886169361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T15:37:48.141506859Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T15:37:48.141484747Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mermaid","state","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zj0es","depends_on_id":"bd-1j2qr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1005,"issue_id":"bd-zj0es","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Already implemented: ZoomIn/ZoomOut with ZOOM_STEP (0.1), ZoomReset to 1.0, FitToView, clamp_zoom() enforcing [0.2, 3.0] range, viewport_pan (i16,i16), viewport_size_override with VIEWPORT_OVERRIDE_MIN_COLS/ROWS clamping, normalize() enforces invariants. Tests: zoom_in, zoom_out, zoom_clamps_max, zoom_clamps_min, zoom_reset, fit_to_view.","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:37:47Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-zkalo","title":"task(effect-executor): introduce an internal executor seam behind Cmd and EffectQueue","description":"Background:
The existing runtime needs an internal seam between public command semantics and the execution substrate. Without that seam, any executor migration becomes a risky cross-cutting rewrite.

Goal:
Introduce a narrow internal executor abstraction behind `Cmd::Task`/effect-queue machinery while preserving the public model contract.","design":"Work packages:
- Identify the exact private surfaces that need abstraction.
- Define executor capabilities: spawn, cancellation, shutdown, deadline/timer hooks, structured result delivery.
- Keep public runtime/model APIs stable.
- Include clear hooks for tracing, metrics, and test harness injection.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Public APIs remain unchanged.
2. The abstraction is small and purpose-built, not a generic over-engineered runtime façade.
3. Unit tests prove the abstraction preserves current command ordering/result semantics.","notes":"Testing requirement:
- Add focused unit tests around task submission, result routing, cancellation, and shutdown edge cases.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T01:18:44.123689472Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T01:19:32.791109418Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync-frankentui","effects","executor","runtime"]}

- Add focused unit tests around task submission, result routing, cancellation, and shutdown edge cases.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T01:18:44.123689472Z","created_by":"ubuntu","assignee":"AmberBadger","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:42:20Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T11:42:20Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync-frankentui","effects","executor","runtime"]}
 {"id":"bd-zkit8","title":"ftui-extras: stabilize input_forwarding_roundtrip PTY exit semantics","description":"The ftui-extras terminal integration test input_forwarding_roundtrip used cat and Ctrl+D to terminate. In CI/coverage this can be flaky depending on PTY line discipline / EOF semantics.  {"id":"bd-zlq9","title":"Add momentum scrolling with velocity tracking","description":"# Add momentum scrolling with velocity tracking

## Background
Part of Progressive Rendering (bd-3t87). Smooth scrolling requires velocity tracking and momentum continuation.

## Technical Design

```rust
impl<T> Virtualized<T> {
    /// Scroll by delta (positive = down)
    pub fn scroll(&mut self, delta: i32) {
        let new_offset = (self.scroll_offset as i32 + delta)
            .max(0)
            .min(self.len().saturating_sub(1) as i32);
        self.scroll_offset = new_offset as usize;
        
        // Cancel follow mode if scrolling up
        if delta < 0 {
            self.follow_mode = false;
        }
    }
    
    /// Scroll to specific item
    pub fn scroll_to(&mut self, idx: usize) {
        self.scroll_offset = idx.min(self.len().saturating_sub(1));
        self.follow_mode = false;
    }
    
    /// Scroll to bottom (enable follow mode)
    pub fn scroll_to_bottom(&mut self) {
        if self.len() > self.visible_count {
            self.scroll_offset = self.len() - self.visible_count;
        }
    }
    
    /// Start momentum scroll with initial velocity
    pub fn fling(&mut self, velocity: f32) {
        self.scroll_velocity = velocity;
        if velocity > 0.0 {
            self.follow_mode = false;
        }
    }
    
    /// Update momentum (call each frame)
    pub fn tick(&mut self, dt: Duration) {
        if self.scroll_velocity.abs() > 0.5 {
            // Apply velocity
            let delta = (self.scroll_velocity * dt.as_secs_f32() * 60.0) as i32;
            self.scroll(delta);
            
            // Apply friction
            self.scroll_velocity *= 0.92;
        } else {
            self.scroll_velocity = 0.0;
        }
    }
    
    /// Enable/disable follow mode
    pub fn set_follow(&mut self, follow: bool) {
        self.follow_mode = follow;
        if follow {
            self.scroll_to_bottom();
        }
    }
    
    /// Check if at bottom
    pub fn is_at_bottom(&self) -> bool {
        self.scroll_offset + self.visible_count >= self.len()
    }
    
    /// Called when items are added
    pub fn on_items_added(&mut self, count: usize) {
        // Update external len if needed
        if let VirtualizedStorage::External { len, .. } = &mut self.items {
            *len += count;
        }
        
        if self.follow_mode {
            self.scroll_to_bottom();
        }
    }
    
    /// Page up/down
    pub fn page_up(&mut self) {
        self.scroll(-(self.visible_count as i32));
    }
    
    pub fn page_down(&mut self) {
        self.scroll(self.visible_count as i32);
    }
    
    /// Home/End
    pub fn scroll_to_start(&mut self) {
        self.scroll_offset = 0;
        self.follow_mode = false;
    }
    
    pub fn scroll_to_end(&mut self) {
        self.scroll_to_bottom();
        self.follow_mode = true;
    }
}

/// Scroll input handling
impl<T> Virtualized<T> {
    pub fn handle_scroll_event(&mut self, event: &Event) {
        match event {
            Event::Mouse(MouseEvent::ScrollUp { .. }) => self.scroll(-3),
            Event::Mouse(MouseEvent::ScrollDown { .. }) => self.scroll(3),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Up, _) => self.scroll(-1),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Down, _) => self.scroll(1),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::PageUp, _) => self.page_up(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::PageDown, _) => self.page_down(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Home, _) => self.scroll_to_start(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::End, _) => self.scroll_to_end(),
            _ => {}
        }
    }
}
```

## Momentum Physics
- Velocity in items/second
- Friction coefficient: 0.92 per frame
- Threshold: stop when |velocity| < 0.5
- Smooth deceleration over ~20 frames

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scroll by delta works
- [ ] Page up/down work
- [ ] Home/End work
- [ ] Momentum scrolling smooth
- [ ] Follow mode auto-scrolls on add
- [ ] Manual scroll cancels follow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:41.092101550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T00:46:29.143532773Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T00:46:29.143444948Z","close_reason":"Momentum scrolling with velocity tracking implemented and tested","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zlq9","depends_on_id":"bd-14s3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
